user_query:
I need to validate and finalize our draft interlocal agreement for shared services, and I want a repeatable way to check that it includes the key legal elements before I present it to our council. Please do the following using only the files under input/ and produce all outputs under reports/:

1) Build a small validator at tools/validate_agreement.py that reads:
   - the draft agreement at input/draft_interlocal_agreement.md,
   - the mandatory clause definitions at input/requirements.json,
   - the allowed statutory citations at input/statutory_refs.json,
   - and the proposed services list at input/proposed_services.csv.

   The validator must:
   - Check the draft for each mandatory clause defined in requirements.json. A clause passes if the agreement contains a section heading that includes the clause’s title (case-insensitive) and the body text contains all listed must_include phrases for that clause (case-insensitive). For the key "statutory_citation", instead of must_include phrases, verify that the agreement contains at least one of the strings listed in statutory_refs.json under allowed_citations.
   - Write a machine-readable compliance report to reports/compliance_report.json with this structure:
     {
       "checked_file": "input/draft_interlocal_agreement.md",
       "summary": {"total": <int>, "passed": <int>, "failed": <int>},
       "details": [
         {"key": "<clause key>", "title": "<title>", "status": "pass"|"fail", "evidence": "<short note or matched text>"},
         ... one per clause ...
       ]
     }
   - Compute service readiness by checking, for each row in input/proposed_services.csv, how many of its required_clauses keys are present in the agreement (using the same clause detection as above). Produce reports/service_readiness.csv with columns:
     rank,service,coverage_ratio,risk_weight,required_clauses,covered_keys
     Rank services by coverage_ratio (descending), breaking ties by lower risk_weight, then by service name ascending.

2) Edit input/draft_interlocal_agreement.md in place to replace placeholders and add any missing clauses so that all validations pass:
   - Replace placeholders with: [LOCAL_GOV] = "Town of Maple Falls", [PARTNER_GOV] = "Pine Ridge Township", [SERVICE_NAME] = "Public Works Equipment Sharing", [EFFECTIVE_DATE] = "2026-07-01".
   - Add a "Term and Termination" section that states a 3-year initial term and allows termination by either party with at least 60 days’ written notice.
   - Add a "Public Records and Open Meetings" section that states the parties will comply with applicable public records and open meetings obligations.
   Ensure the agreement still cites at least one allowed statute from input/statutory_refs.json.

3) Run your validator as a command (this is essential):
   python tools/validate_agreement.py --agreement input/draft_interlocal_agreement.md --requirements input/requirements.json --statutes input/statutory_refs.json --services input/proposed_services.csv --out reports

   After your edits, the compliance report should show all clauses as "pass", and the readiness file should reflect the final agreement’s coverage.

4) Compose a brief status update at reports/mayor_update.md that includes:
   - A short summary of the changes you made to the agreement (list the sections you added/edited).
   - A one-line test outcome summary drawn from reports/compliance_report.json (e.g., "8 checked, 8 passed, 0 failed").
   - A "Top 3 Services by Readiness" section listing the top three services in the order shown in reports/service_readiness.csv, each with its coverage_ratio and risk_weight.

Constraints and notes:
- Do not hardcode clause titles or citations; always read them from the local input files.
- The validator must rely on the current on-disk content of input/draft_interlocal_agreement.md to compute results.
- All outputs must be written under the reports/ directory exactly as named above, and the edited agreement must remain at input/draft_interlocal_agreement.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
